{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Papua New Guinea women's national soccer team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=December 2020}}
{{Use Australian English|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Papua New Guinea
| Badge              = PNGFA Logo.svg
| Badge_size         = 155px
| Nickname           = ''Kapuls'' ([[Cuscus]])
| Association        = [[Papua New Guinea Football Association]] (PNGFA)
| Confederation      = [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (Oceania)
| Coach              = [[David Muta]]
| Captain            = [[Alwin Komolong]]
| Most caps          = [[Emmanuel Simon]] (41)
| Top scorer         = [[Raymond Gunemba]] (18)
| Home Stadium       = [[Hubert Murray Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = PNG
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|PNG}}
| FIFA max           = 153
| FIFA max date      = June 2017
| FIFA min           = 206
| FIFA min date      = October–November 2015
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Papua New Guinea}}
| Elo max            = 134
| Elo max date       = 1989
| Elo min            = 188
| Elo min date       = 2003
| pattern_la1        = 
| pattern_b1         = _PNG2024
| pattern_ra1        = 
| pattern_sh1        = 
| pattern_so1        = 
| leftarm1           = 000000
| body1              = FF0000
| rightarm1          = 000000
| shorts1            = FF0000
| socks1             = FF0000
| pattern_la2        = 
| pattern_b2         = _PNG2024
| pattern_ra2        = 
| pattern_sh2        = 
| leftarm2           = 000000
| body2              = FFC90E
| rightarm2          = 000000
| shorts2            = FFC90E
| socks2             = FFC90E
| First game         = {{nowrap|{{fb|FIJ|colonial}} 3–1 '''Papua and New Guinea''' {{flagdeco|Territory of Papua and New Guinea|code=PNG}}}}<br /> ([[Suva]], Fiji; 29 August 1963)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesp/png-intres.html|title=Papua New Guinea International Matches|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=29 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220929134500/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesp/png-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
| Largest win        = {{nowrap|{{fb|PNG}} 20–0 {{fb-rt|ASA}}}}<br /> ([[Nouméa]], New Caledonia; 15 December 1987)
| Largest loss       = {{nowrap|{{fb|MAS}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|PNG}}}}<br /> ([[Kuala Terengganu]], Malaysia; 20 June 2023)
| Regional name      = [[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
| Regional cup apps  = 5
| Regional cup first = [[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980]]
| Regional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]])
|3rdRegional cup apps=9|3rdRegional name=[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesia Cup / MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[1989 Melanesia Cup|1989]]|3rdRegional cup best=[[Melanesia Cup]]: Third place <br />([[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994]])<br /> [[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]: '''Champions'''<br />([[2022 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2022]], [[2024 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2024]])

| medaltemplates = {{MedalSport|Men's [[association football|football]]}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]}}
{{Medal|RU|[[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016 Papua New Guinea]]|}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969 Papua New Guinea]]|}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987 New Caledonia]]|}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[Football at the Pacific Mini Games|Pacific Mini Games]]}}
{{Medal|Bronze|[[Football at the 1981 South Pacific Mini Games|1981 Solomon Islands]]|}}
{{MedalComp|[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesian Cup/MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]}}
{{Medal|W|[[2022 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2022 Vanuatu]]|}}
{{Medal|W|[[2024 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2024 Solomon Islands]]|}}
{{Medal|RU|[[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2025 Papua New Guinea]]|}}
{{Medal|3rd|[[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994 Solomon Islands]]|}}
}}

The '''Papua New Guinea national soccer team''' is the men's national [[Association football|soccer]] team of [[Papua New Guinea]] and is controlled by the [[Papua New Guinea Football Association]]. Its nickname is the ''Kapuls'',<ref>{{cite news |url = http://www.brisbanestrikers.com.au/?p=1236 |title = Strikers To Play Papua New Guinea |publisher = Steve Pitman, [[Brisbane Strikers]] |date = 30 May 2011 |access-date = 31 March 2012 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120318213651/http://www.brisbanestrikers.com.au/?p=1236 |archive-date = 18 March 2012 |url-status = dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url = http://touch.fifa.com/newscentre/news/newsid=1632977.html | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120802162454/http://touch.fifa.com/newscentre/news/newsid=1632977.html | url-status = dead | archive-date = 2 August 2012 |title = Farina's PNG aiming to defy odds |publisher = [[FIFA|Fédération Internationale de Football Association]] (FIFA) |date=18 May 2012 |access-date =18 May 2012}}</ref> which is [[Tok Pisin]] for [[Cuscus]].

Papua New Guinea's highest ever [[FIFA]] [[FIFA World Rankings|ranking]] was 153, in June 2017. As of April 2024, the country was ranked 166 out of 211 countries. This was a drop of 1 place from February 2024.<ref name="FIFA/Coca-Cola-World-Rankings">{{cite web |last1=FIFA/Coca-Cola-World-Rankings |first1=FIFA/Coca-Cola-World-Rankings |title=FIFA/Coca-Cola-World-Rankings |url=https://inside.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/men?dateId=id14338 |website=Men's Ranking |access-date=23 April 2024}}</ref> Papua New Guinea had previously left the FIFA rankings, having not competed in a match between July 2007 and August 2011. Their matches at the [[Men's Football at the 2015 Pacific Games|2015 Pacific Games]] saw them return to the rankings, and they competed in the [[2016 OFC Nations Cup]] in June 2016; they reached [[2016 OFC Nations Cup Final|the final]], but lost to [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]] 4–2 on [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalties]] after the score was tied at 0–0 after [[Overtime (sports)#Association football|extra time]].

==History==

===1963–1978===

The Papuan national team played its first match at the [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963 South Pacific Games]], where it fell to [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] 3-1 and was eliminated. At the [[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966 South Pacific Games]] they reached the third place match but lost to the [[New Hebrides national football team|New Hebrides]]. Three years later they reached the same stage, and defeated the Fijian team 2-1 to win the bronze medal.

At [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971 South Pacific Games]], after losing in the semi-finals to [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]], they were beaten 8-1 by [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]] in the third place game. Four years later, at the 1975 Games, they were beaten in the group stage by Tahiti and New Caledonia and thus eliminated in the first round.

===1979–1995===
At the [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979 South Pacific Games]] they lost in the quarter-finals 3–2 against the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]], and in the first phase of the consolation tournament they were beaten 2–0 at the hands of the [[New Hebrides national football team|New Hebrides]]. The following year they played in the [[1980 Oceania Cup]], the precursor to the [[OFC Nations Cup]], where despite beating the New Hebrides team, their losses to [[Australia men's national soccer team|Australia]] and [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]] left them out at the first phase.

At [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983 South Pacific Games]] they lost the match for third place against the New Caledonian team. In the 1987 edition they won the bronze medal again by beating [[Vanuatu national football team|Vanuatu]] 3-1. Even so, in the two subsequent editions, 1991 and 1995, they were eliminated in the first phase.

===1996–2012===
After an absence in three editions of the OFC Nations Cup, due to the poor results obtained in the [[Melanesia Cup]], they qualified for the [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002 tournament]]. They were only able to salvage a point in a 0-0 draw against the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] and bowed out in the first round. In [[Football at the 2003 South Pacific Games|2003 South Pacific Games]] they had a poor performance and in five games only obtained four points.

After not participating in 2007, which served as qualification for the [[2008 OFC Nations Cup]], they were eliminated in the first phase at [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011 Pacific Games]] by having worse goal difference than [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]], with whom they tied on points. In the [[2012 OFC Nations Cup]] they drew with [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] and lost to the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] and [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]], again being eliminated in the group stage.

===2013–present=== 

[[File:Alwin Komolong.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Alwin Komolong]] played numerous football matches for Papua New Guinea]]

In 2015, Papua New Guinea was designated to host the [[2016 OFC Nations Cup]]. In the group stage, the Papuan team drew 1–1 with [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]], 2–2 against [[Tahiti national football team|Tahiti]] and beat [[Samoa national football team|Samoa]] 8–0. After achieving victory in the semi-finals, 2–1 against the Solomon Islands, they lost on penalties to [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]] in the final after drawing 0–0 in regulation time. On 20 June 2023, Papua New Guinea suffered their worst defeat in their history losing to [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] 10–0 at the [[Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin Stadium]].

==== FIFA Series tournament ====
In 2024, Papua New Guinea played their first ever tournament outside Oceania where [[FIFA]] invited them to the [[2024 FIFA Series]] matches on from 22 to 25 March 2024 held in [[Colombo]] where they would face [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] and [[Central African Republic national football team|Central African Republic]].

==Kit sponsorship==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
|-
! Kit supplier
! Period
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[Joma]] ||1998–2000<ref>{{Cite web|url = https://www.oldfootballshirts.com/pt/teams/p/papua-new-guinea/old-papua-new-guinea-football-shirt-s70265.html|title = Fake & Counterfeit Shirts from all over camisa de futebol 1998 - 2000|date = 12 August 2019|access-date = 4 April 2020|archive-date = 25 November 2021|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20211125005021/https://www.oldfootballshirts.com/pt/teams/p/papua-new-guinea/old-papua-new-guinea-football-shirt-s70265.html|url-status = live}}</ref>
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Puma (brand)|Puma]] ||2007–2010
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] || 2011
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|United States}} [[Nike, Inc|Nike]]|| 2012–2014
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Unknown}} OFC|| 2014
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} [[Kukuri]] || 2015
|-
|None || 2016
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Adidas]] || 2017–2023
|-
|align="left"|{{flagicon|Australia}} Veto || 2024–
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Papua New Guinea national soccer team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

=== 2025 ===
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Group A|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]
| date       = 13 November
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|10}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|PNG}}
| score      = 1–0
| report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/papua-new-guinea-OjkU0PHe/solomon-islands-fF5OOS92/?mid=40TCUcRF
| team2      = {{fb|SOL}}
| goals1     = *[[Pala Paul|P. Paul]] {{goal|75}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[PNG Football Stadium]]
| location   = [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Semi-finals|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup semi-final]]
| date       = 20 November
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|10}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|PNG}}
| score      = 2–0
| team2      = {{fb|FIJ}}
| goals1     = *[[Raymond Gunemba|Gunemba]] {{Goal|57||64}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[PNG Football Stadium]]
| location   = [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
| attendance =
| referee    =
| report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/fiji-hW4TJi0B/papua-new-guinea-OjkU0PHe/?mid=QkAJpysI
| result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament#Final|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup final]]
| date       = 23 November
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|10}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|PNG}}
| score      = 2–2
| team2      = {{fb|VAN}}
| goals1     = *[[Ati Kepo|Kepo]] {{goal|56}}
*[[Pala Paul|Paul]] {{goal|101}}
| goals2     = *[[Alex Saniel|A. Saniel]] {{goal|20}}
*[[Luka Tioni|L. Tioni]] {{goal|94}}
|penaltyscore= 4–5
| stadium    = [[Sir John Guise Stadium]]
| location   = [[Port Moresby]], Papua New Guinea
| attendance =
| referee    =
| report     = https://news.pngfacts.com/2025/11/vanuatu-stun-png-in-port-moresby-to.html
| result     = D
}}

==Coaching history==
{{div col|colwidth=17em}}
*{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Richard Tamari Nagai]] (1996–1998)
*{{flagicon|PNG}} [[John Davani]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|NZL}} [[Steve Cain]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Ludwig Peka]] (2003–2004)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Marcos Gusmão]] (2004–2011)
*{{flagicon|AUS}} [[Frank Farina]] (2011–2013)
*{{flagicon|USA}} [[Mike Keeney]] (2013)
*{{flagicon|NZL}} [[Wynton Rufer]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|DEN}} [[Flemming Serritslev]] (2015–2018)
*{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Bob Morris (football manager)|Bob Morris]] (2019–2021)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Marcos Gusmão]] (2021–2022)
*{{flagicon|AUS}} [[Warren Moon (soccer)|Warren Moon]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|ESP}} [[Felipe Vega-Arango]] (2024–2025)
*{{flagicon|PNG}} [[David Muta]] (2025–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] against [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]] on 14 November 2024 and against [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] on 17 November 2024.<ref>{{cite web|title=Squad Announcement|url=https://www.facebook.com/pngfa2020/posts/pfbid0ETzLRarVUwYMQ6pwfu4j2kpwXjssEwuGebTwik4z77SyTsfVcBCjzeoHfKfTZ7B9l|website=Facebook|publisher=Papua New Guinea Football Association|date=8 November 2024}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 17 November 2024, after the match against [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]].''

{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Dave Tomare]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|4|26|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Charles Lepani (footballer)|Charles Lepani]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|8|20|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=Papua New Guinea}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Christinus Biasu]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|6|2|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Daniel Joe]]|age={{birth date and age|1990|5|29|df=y}}|caps=37|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Alwin Komolong]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|11|2|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=2|club=[[Queensland Lions FC|Queensland Lions]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kolu Kepo]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|7|15|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=3|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=Papua New Guinea}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Joshua Talau]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|4|19|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=Papua New Guinea}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nathaniel Eddie]]|age=''unknown''|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Arol Tateng]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|4|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Southern Strikers F.C.|Southern Strikers]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Derek Kimai]]|age=''unknown''|caps=2|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Rex Naime]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|10|23|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Simon Oberth]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|1|1|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Joseph Joe]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|6|14|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Solomon Rani]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|5|22|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Shane Sakael]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|12|31|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Juduh Asar]]|age=''unknown''|caps=2|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ritzoki Tamgol]]|age=''unknown''|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[F.C. Port Moresby|Port Moresby]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Chappy Upaiga]]|age=''unknown''|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ethan Mom]]|age=''unknown''|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Raymond Gunemba]]|age={{Birth date and age|1986|6|4|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=15|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tommy Semmy]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|9|30|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=10|club=[[Melbourne Knights FC|Melbourne Knights]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ati Kepo]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|1|15|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=8|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Logan Biwa]]|age=''unknown''|caps=1|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Papua New Guinea squad within the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r start}}
<!-- {{nat fs break}} -->
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ronald Warisan]]|age={{birth date and age|1989|9|20|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Vagi Koniel]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|10|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Southern Strikers F.C.|Southern Strikers]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Godfrey Haro]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|6|30|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=Papua New Guinea|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Felix Komolong]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|3|6|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Lennard Atterwell]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|11|21|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Brisbane Roar FC|Brisbane Roar]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Raymon Diho]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|1|1|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[North Geelong Warriors FC|North Geelong Warriors]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Philip Steven]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|1|19|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[F.C. Port Moresby|Port Moresby]]|clubnat=Papua New Guinea|latest=[[2024 FIFA Series]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Joseph Waiwai]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|3|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Southern Strikers F.C.|Port Moresby Strikers]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 FIFA Series]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Emmanuel Simon]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|12|25|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=4|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Yagi Yasasa]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|8|17|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kenneth Arah]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|1|15|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Gulf Komara F.C.|Gulf Komara]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bruce Tiampo]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|7|25|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=v. {{fb|NCL}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Pala Paul]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|7|25|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[Southern Strikers F.C.|Southern Strikers]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Troy Dobbin]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|9|24|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Queensland Lions FC|Queensland Lions]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Lee-Navu Faunt]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|5|4|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Redlands United FC|Redlands United]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jethro Yumange]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|3|10|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[River Light FC|River Light]]|clubnat=USA|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nigel Dabinyaba]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|10|26|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=11|club=[[Hekari United]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 FIFA Series]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Matu Ben]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|1|31|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Lae City F.C.|Lae City]]|clubnat=PNG|latest=[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|20 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Papua New Guinea |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/143/Papua_New_Guinea.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=4 March 2022 |archive-date=29 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230329090943/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/143/2023/Papua_New_Guinea.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
: Statistics include official FIFA-recognised matches only.
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Papua New Guinea.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:175px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|'''[[Emmanuel Simon]]'''{{efn|Simon also played twice against Vanuatu B, matches that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|41
|4
|2014–present
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Daniel Joe]]'''{{efn|Joe also played twice against Vanuatu B, matches that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|39
|1
|2012–present
|-
|3
|align=left|'''[[Raymond Gunemba]]'''{{efn|Gunemba also played twice against Vanuatu B, scoring once, matches and goals that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|37
|18
|2012–present
|-
|4
|align=left|'''[[Ronald Warisan]]'''{{efn|Warisan also played twice against Vanuatu B, matches that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|36
|0
|2014–present
|-
|5
|align=left|[[Michael Foster (footballer)|Michael Foster]]
|31
|7
|2003–2022
|-
|6
|align=left|'''[[Alwin Komolong]]'''{{efn|Komolong also played once against Vanuatu B, a match that is not considered official by FIFA.}}
|30
|3
|2016–present
|-
|7
|align=left|'''[[Tommy Semmy]]'''
|27
|10
|2014–present
|-
|8
|align="left"|'''[[Kolu Kepo]]'''
|26
|4
|2016–present
|-
|9
|align="left"|'''[[Ati Kepo]]'''
|25
|9
|2019–present
|-
|10
|align=left|'''[[Felix Komolong]]'''
|23
|0
|2016–present
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=175px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Raymond Gunemba]]'''{{efn|Gunemba also played twice against Vanuatu B, scoring once, matches and goals that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|18
|37
|{{#expr:18/37 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Reggie Davani]]
|13
|17
|{{#expr:13/17 round 2}}
|2002–2012
|-
|3
|align="left"|'''[[Tommy Semmy]]'''
|10
|27
|{{#expr:10/27 round 2}}
|2014–present
|-
|rowspan=2|4
|align="left"|'''[[Nigel Dabinyaba]]'''{{efn|Dabinyaba also played once against Vanuatu B, scoring twice, a match and goals that are not considered official by FIFA.}}
|9
|22
|{{#expr:9/22 round 2}}
|2014–present
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Ati Kepo]]'''
|9
|25
|{{#expr:9/25 round 2}}
|2019–present
|-
|6
|align="left"|[[Michael Foster (footballer)|Michael Foster]]
|7
|31
|{{#expr:7/31 round 2}}
|2003–2022
|-
|7
|align="left"|[[Nathaniel Lepani]]
|6
|8
|{{#expr:6/8 round 2}}
|2004–2011
|-
|rowspan=3|8
|align="left"|[[Neil Hans]]
|5
|8
|{{#expr:5/8 round 2}}
|2007–2014
|-
|align="left"|[[Andrew Lepani]]
|5
|12
|{{#expr:5/12 round 2}}
|2003–2011
|-
|align="left"|[[Mauri Wasi]]
|5
|14
|{{#expr:5/14 round 2}}
|2002–2014
|-
|}

'''Notes'''
{{notelist}}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan="15"|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Abbr|Pos|Position}}
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=7|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]] to [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|3rd
|6
|2
|1
|3
|5
|13
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR|1997}} {{flagicon|JPN}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|3rd
|4
|2
|1
|1
|17
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|colspan=8|''Disqualified''
| colspan="7" |''Disqualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="4" |''Did not qualify''
|4th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|2
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|RUS}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|3rd 
|9
|3
|3
|3
|19
|13
|-
|{{flagicon|QAT}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|3rd 
|4
|2
|0
|2
|5
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}} {{flagicon|MEX}} {{flagicon|USA}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|7th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|5
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}}{{flagicon|POR}}{{flagicon|ESP}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined'' 
| colspan="7" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|KSA}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/14|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!
!29
!9
!7
!13
!53
!49
|}

<nowiki>*</nowiki> Although initially listed by FIFA as having entered the 2010 World Cup, PNG did not enter the [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|football tournament at the 2007 South Pacific Games]], which was used as the preliminary round of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)|Oceanian zone qualification tournament]].

===OFC Nations Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
! colspan="10"|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|Oceania Cup / OFC Nations Cup]] record
!rowspan="14"|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} [[1973 OFC Nations Cup|1973]]
|colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''No qualification''
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|french}} [[1980 OFC Nations Cup|1980]]
|Group stage
|6th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|6
|22
|{{n/a}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[1996 OFC Nations Cup|1996]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|1
|2
|2
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[1998 OFC Nations Cup|1998]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|3
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[2000 OFC Nations Cup|2000]]
|4
|0
|0
|4
|4
|19
|-
|{{flagicon|New Zealand}} [[2002 OFC Nations Cup|2002]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|2
|12
|[[2002 OFC Nations Cup squads#Papua New Guinea|Squad]]
|4
|4
|0
|0
|20
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[2004 OFC Nations Cup|2004]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|4
|2
|1
|1
|17
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Pacific Community}} [[2008 OFC Nations Cup|2008]]
|colspan=9|''Disqualified''
|colspan=6|''Disqualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[2012 OFC Nations Cup|2012]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|2
|4
|[[2012 OFC Nations Cup squads#Papua New Guinea|Squad]]
|colspan=6 rowspan=3|''Qualified automatically''
|- style="background:silver"
|style="border: 3px solid red"| {{flagicon|Papua New Guinea}} [[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]]
|[[2016 OFC Nations Cup Final|Runners-up]]
|2nd
|5
|2
|3
|0
|13
|4
|[[2016 OFC Nations Cup squads#Papua New Guinea|Squad]]
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} {{flagicon|VAN}} [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]
|Group stage
|5th
|3
|1
|1
|1
|4
|7
|[[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup squads#Papua New Guinea|Squad]]
|-
!Total
!Runners-up
!5/11
!17
!4
!6
!7
!27
!49
!—
!20
!8
!3
!9
!46
!37
|}
:''*Denotes draws include knockout matches decided via [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty shoot-out]].''

===Pacific Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
! colspan="9"|[[Pacific Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ|colonial}} [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]]
|1st round||5th||1||0||0||1||1||3
|- 
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966]]
|Fourth place||4th||4||1||0||3||14||14
|- style="background:#CC9966;"
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|PNG|1965}} [[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969]]
|Third place||3rd||6||3||1||2||11||10
|- 
|{{flagicon|TAH|1946}} [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
|Fourth place||4th||3||1||1||1||19||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Guam}} [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]]
|Group stage||6th||2||0||0||2||3||9
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]]
|Quarter-finals||5th||3||1||1||1||15||3
|- 
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983]]
|Fourth place||4th||4||1||0||3||22||10
|- style="background:#CC9966;"
|{{flagicon|NCL|french}} [[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987]]
|Third place||3rd||6||3||2||1||26||4
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 1991 South Pacific Games|1991]]
|Group stage||5th||3||1||1||1||3||2
|-
|{{flagicon|TAH}} [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]]
|Group stage||6th||3||1||1||1||11||5
|-
|{{flagicon|FIJ}} [[Football at the 2003 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2003]]
|Group stage||6th||4||1||1||2||12||7
|-
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2007]]
|colspan="8"|''Disqualified''
|-
|{{flagicon|NCL|football}} [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011]]
|Group stage||6th||4||2||1||1||22||4
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Football at the 2015 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2015]]
|colspan="8"|''See [[Papua New Guinea national under-23 football team|Papua New Guinea national under-23 team]]''
|- 
|{{flagicon|SAM}} [[Football at the 2019 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2019]]
|Fourth place||4th||5||3||1||1||17||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Football at the 2023 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
|Group stage||6th||4||2||1||1||7||3
|-
!Total!!Third place!!14/16!!52!!20!!11!!30!!183!!88
|}

===MSG Prime Minister's Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
! colspan="9"|[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[1988 Melanesia Cup|1988]]||colspan="8"|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[1989 Melanesia Cup|1989]]||Fourth place||4th||4||1||1||2||5||4
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|football}} [[1990 Melanesia Cup|1990]]||Fifth place||5th||4||0||0||4||1||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Vanuatu}} [[1992 Melanesia Cup|1992]]||colspan="8"|''Did not enter''
|-style="background:#CC9966;"
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994]]||Third place||3rd||4||1||1||2||2||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Vanuatu}} [[1998 Melanesia Cup|1998]]||Fourth place||4th||4||1||1||2||3||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[2000 Melanesia Cup|2000]]||Fifth place||5th||4||0||0||4||4||19
|-bgcolor=gold 
|{{flagicon|Vanuatu}} [[2022 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2022]]||Champions||1st||4||3||1||0||7||4
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|football}} [[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2023]]||Fourth place||4th||3||0||0||3||2||7
|-bgcolor=gold
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[2024 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2024]]||Champions||1st||4||3||1||0||7||4
|-bgcolor=silver
|style="border: 3px solid red" |{{flagicon|Papua New Guinea}} [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament|2025]]
|Second place||2nd||3||2||1||0||5||2
|-
!Total
!Participations
!9/11
!34
!11
!6
!17
!36
!55
|}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[OFC Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2016 OFC Nations Cup|2016]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medal (2): [[1969 Pacific Games |1969]], [[1987 South Pacific Games |1987]]
* '''[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesia Cup / MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (2):''' [[2022 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2022]], [[2024 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2024]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament|2025]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[1994 Melanesia Cup|1994]]

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
|0||1||0||1
|-
!Total!!0!!1!!0!!1
|}

==Head-to-head record==
{{#lst:Papua New Guinea national football team results|Record by opponent}}

==Historical kits==
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _PNG1996H
| pattern_b   = _PNG1996H
| pattern_ra  = _PNG1996H
| pattern_sh  = _vertical_thin_stripes
| pattern_so  = _stripe_white
| leftarm     = FF0000
| body        = FF0000
| rightarm    = FF0000
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title       = 1996 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _PNG2002H
| pattern_b   = _PNG2002H
| pattern_ra  = _PNG2002H
| pattern_sh  = _vertical_thin_stripes
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = FF0000
| body        = FF0000
| rightarm    = FF0000
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = FFFF00
| title       = 2002 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _puma_2004_red
| pattern_b   = _puma_2004_red
| pattern_ra  = _puma_2004_red
| pattern_sh  = _pumawhite
| pattern_so  = _2_red_stripes
| leftarm     = FFFF00
| body        = FFFF00
| rightarm    = FFFF00
| shorts      = FF0000
| socks       = FFFF00
| title       = 2004
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _Nike_white_transparent
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _nikewhite
| pattern_so  = _nikewhite
| leftarm     = FF0000
| body        = FF0000
| rightarm    = FF0000
| shorts      = FF0000
| socks       = FF0000
| title       = 2011 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _PNG2014H
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = FF0000
| body        = FF0000
| rightarm    = FF0000
| shorts      = FF0000
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2014 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _PNG2014A
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 000000
| body        = 000000
| rightarm    = 000000
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title       = 2014 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _OFC2015black
| pattern_b   = _OFC2015black
| pattern_ra  = _OFC2015black
| pattern_sh  = _black_border
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = D8000A
| body        = D8000A
| rightarm    = D8000A
| shorts      = D8000A
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2015 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _OFC2015black
| pattern_b   = _OFC2015black
| pattern_ra  = _OFC2015black
| pattern_sh  = _yellow_border
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = FFFF00
| body        = FFFF00
| rightarm    = FFFF00
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2015 Away
}}
|}
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _png17h
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _adidaswhite
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_white
| leftarm     = FF0000
| body        = 
| rightarm    = FF0000
| shorts      = FF0000
| socks       = FF0000
| title       = 2017 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _adidas_white
| pattern_b   = _adidas_hem_white
| pattern_ra  = _adidas_white
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = _3_stripes_black
| leftarm     = 000000
| body        = 000000
| rightarm    = 000000
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = FFF000
| title       = 2017 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _png22h
| pattern_b   = _png22h
| pattern_ra  = _png22h
| pattern_sh  = _png22h
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FF0000
| title       = 2022 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _png22a
| pattern_b   = _png22a
| pattern_ra  = _png22a
| pattern_sh  = _png22a
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title       = 2022 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _yellowborder
| pattern_b   = _png22t
| pattern_ra  = _yellowborder
| pattern_sh  = _png22a
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = EE0000
| body        = 
| rightarm    = EE0000
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FFF000
| title       = 2022 Third
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _PNG2024
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 000000
| body        = FF0000
| rightarm    = 000000
| shorts      = FF0000
| socks       = FF0000
| title       = 2024 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _PNG2024
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = 
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 000000
| body        = FFC90E
| rightarm    = 000000
| shorts      = FFC90E
| socks       = FFC90E
| title       = 2024 Away
}}
|}

Sources:<ref>{{cite web | url= https://anotandofutbol.blogspot.com/search?q=papua+nueva+guinea | title= FOOTBALL IN PAPUA NEW GUINEA | date= 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url= https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nOGB4FvsSus | title= 2014 | website= [[YouTube]] | date= 6 September 2014 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url= https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QsB4Q8K6Kpw | title= 2017 | website= [[YouTube]] | date= 29 March 2017 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url= https://www.gettyimages.es/detail/fotograf%C3%ADa-de-noticias/phillip-steven-of-papua-new-guinea-during-the-fotograf%C3%ADa-de-noticias/2104595714?adppopup=true| title= 2024 | date= 22 March 2024 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url= https://pina.com.fj/2024/03/26/central-african-republic-too-strong-for-papua-new-guinea-in-fifa-series/ | title= 2024 | date= 26 March 2024 }}</ref>

==See also==
* [[Papua New Guinea women's national soccer team]]

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
* [http://www.pngfootball.com.pg/ Papua New Guinea Football Association]
* [https://www.oceaniafootball.com/papua-new-guinea/ Papua New Guinea] at [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/PNG Papua New Guinea] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.oceaniafootball.com/png-land-iran-friendly/ National football team of Papua New Guinea picture]

{{Papua New Guinea national football team}}
{{Navboxes
| title = Links to related articles
| bg        = #C8102E
| fg        = #FFCD00
| bordercolor = black
| list =
{{Navboxes
| title = Papua New Guinea squads
| bg        = #C8102E
| fg        = #FFCD00
| bordercolor = black
| list =
{{Papua New Guinea squad 2002 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Papua New Guinea squad 2012 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Papua New Guinea squad 2016 OFC Nations Cup}}
{{Papua New Guinea squad 2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup}}
}}
{{Football in Papua New Guinea}}
{{Football head to head}}
{{OFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of Papua New Guinea}}
}}
{{Portal bar|Soccer|Oceania}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Papua New Guinea Men's National Football Team}}
[[Category:Papua New Guinea national soccer team| ]]
[[Category:Papua New Guinea men's national soccer teams| ]]
[[Category:Oceanian national association football teams]]
